steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The strongest signal in the text is the transitional shift between the first phrase about Richard’s intended effect and the second phrase explaining how he achieves it (“assembles…sculptures”). This implies the first blank needs to show intent or purpose, with proper grammatical structure.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: C uses a participial phrase (“intending”) to describe Richard Serra’s actions and logically connects to the main verb in the second clause (“assembles”). It avoids splitting two independent clauses with a comma (a comma splice) by transforming the first clause into a non-finite participial modifier. The other choices split two independent clauses with a comma alone (A, B, D), violating sentence-boundary rules. That matches choice C — C is correct.
